body:
9.38 MILESTONE: compiled clean (0 errors, 0 warnings, verified via metaeditor.exe against the live Experts folder copy -- confirmed 9.37 itself also compiles clean as a baseline first). Code and compile only, no MT4/chart interaction, as instructed.

WHAT WAS DONE:

1. ComponentWeights struct restored to 9 independent fields (mtf, momentum, volatility, volume, session, pattern, reversal, directional, predictive) per your decision. Fixed every call site that had the collision bug -- OnInit, live adaptive weight reduction/recovery/amplification, CATREC component damping, file save, file load, Discovery-apply, HistoricalBacktester-apply, SmartCB save/restore (both directions). trend->mtf was a clean 1:1 rename with no data loss, left as-is (just stripped the stale comment).

2. BONUS FIND while fixing this: there's a SECOND, previously-undiscovered instance of the exact same bug in a different save block inside SaveSystemState() (line ~23601) that didn't have the '/* was: X */' marker comment, so it didn't show up in my original grep. Fixed that one too.

3. DEEPER ISSUE FOUND, NOT YET FIXED: SaveSystemState() appears to write the discovery-weights + component-weights block TWICE within itself (once ~23258, again ~23594-23606), but LoadSystemState() only reads that block ONCE. If that's a genuine duplicate write (not intentional versioning), every FileReadDouble() call after that point in LoadSystemState() would be reading from the wrong file offset -- a much bigger issue than the value-collision bug, since it could misalign ALL subsequent persisted data, not just weights. This needs careful field-by-field reconciliation of both ~500-line functions, not a guess-fix -- flagging it rather than touching it blind given the stakes of getting file I/O wrong. Want me to do that reconciliation as part of 9.39?

4. regimeStrategies[9-11]: left untouched, added a comment block explaining they're reserved for the unfinished adaptive/hybrid regime concept and are not currently reachable by the live classifier.

5. Dynamic growth rate implemented: new function CalculateDefensibleDailyGrowthRate() replaces the dead MED_DAILY_GROWTH_RATE/MED_MEDIAN_RETURN constants (confirmed they were never read anywhere in the file). It reuses the EXACT half-Kelly formula from CalculateOptimalPositionSizing() (not a separate guess), fed by CalculateRecentWinRate() and tpslSystem.recentRR (both live, already-tracked values), compounded at the tier's target trades/day (FLOOR_TRADES_PER_DAY=5 or MED_TRADES_PER_DAY=10). Wired into E99_CalculatePathToMillion(), logs hourly: 'GROWTH | defensible daily X% | 45-day projection \ -> \ | WR% RR half-Kelly'. This will report whatever the system's ACTUAL live edge supports -- likely low single digits per day, not 42%, until real trade history says otherwise.

6. SEPARATE BUG NOTICED (not fixed, flagging): E99_CalculatePathToMillion() line ~52687 computes 'days so far' as (TimeCurrent() - g_E99_Milestones.absoluteStartBalance) / 86400 -- that's subtracting a dollar balance from a Unix timestamp. There's no start-TIME field on that struct to fix it against without adding one. This corrupts the 'estimated days to \' display. Want this fixed in 9.39 (would need a new struct field + populate at OnInit)?

Ready to proceed to 9.39 (coherence pass) once you confirm on items 3 and 6, or I can proceed with my own judgment on both (fix the PathToMillion field bug by adding a start-time field; do the SaveSystemState/LoadSystemState reconciliation carefully) if you'd rather not wait. Your call given how much money-adjacent file I/O correctness matters here.
